paper_title: Sediment Core Analysis and Vegetation History Reconstruction

methods.description: This method involves obtaining radiocarbon dates for sediment core samples and using these dates to build age-depth models, which are essential for understanding the chronological framework of the sediment cores.

steps.description: A total of nine accelerator mass spectrometry radiocarbon dates were obtained for bulk subsamples from the sediment cores from DirectAMS, Washington.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain radiocarbon dates for sediment core samples

steps.description: Resulting calibrated radiocarbon dates (based on SHCal20) were used to build age-depth models for the cores in R Bacon.
steps.searchable_summary: build age-depth models using calibrated radiocarbon dates in R Bacon

methods.description: This method involves analyzing sediment cores for pollen and spores to reconstruct historical vegetation changes, using standard preparation protocols and statistical analysis.

steps.description: Each sediment core was analyzed for pollen and spores at 4-8 cm intervals to reconstruct forest and moorland history.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sediment cores for pollen and spores at specified intervals

steps.description: Sample preparation follows the standard protocol, which involves HCl, KOH and Acetolysis treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples using HCl, KOH, and Acetolysis treatment

steps.description: A minimum of 300 terrestrial taxa were counted and identified in each pollen sample and results were presented as percentages calculated based on the sum of trees, shrubs, and herbs.
steps.searchable_summary: count and identify terrestrial taxa in pollen samples and calculate percentages

steps.description: Turnover analysis was performed on pollen data using the Squared Chord Dissimilarity (SCD) in R 'analogue' package.
steps.searchable_summary: perform turnover analysis using SCD in R 'analogue' package

methods.description: This method involves analyzing sediment cores for macroscopic charcoal to reconstruct fire history, using bleaching, sieving, and statistical analysis.

steps.description: Sediment cores were contiguously sampled for macroscopic charcoal analysis at 1 cm interval.
steps.searchable_summary: sample sediment cores for macroscopic charcoal at 1 cm intervals

steps.description: Sediment samples were bleached and sieved through a 125-µm sieve before counting macroscopic charcoal particles.
steps.searchable_summary: bleach and sieve sediment samples, then count charcoal particles

steps.description: CharAnalysis program was used to calculate fire return interval (FRI) based on the frequency of charcoal peaks.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate fire return interval using CharAnalysis program
